message:
refactor: simplify configure command by removing --section flag

Streamlines configuration editing with single comprehensive flow:

Removed Complexity:
- No more --section flag (runtime, credentials, container, all)
- Single configuration workflow instead of 4 different flows
- Simpler command interface: just `packnplay configure`
- Cleaner help text and documentation

Simplified UX:
- Single command shows all settings in logical flow
- Users get complete visibility into their configuration
- Fewer command variations to learn and remember
- Still preserves all safety guarantees for unshown settings

Updated Documentation:
- README reflects simplified command structure
- Help text emphasizes logical flow approach
- Tests verify no section flag exists
- Clear documentation about preservation behavior

Benefits:
- Easier to use - no flags to remember
- Better visibility - users see all their current settings
- Simpler codebase - one workflow instead of multiple
- Same safety guarantees as before

The YAGNI principle applies - section-specific editing was unnecessary
complexity when most users want to see all their settings anyway.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

files.patch:
@@ -345,17 +345,15 @@ packnplay mounts your project at the **exact same path** inside the container as
 
 **Reconfigure packnplay safely:**
 ```bash
-packnplay configure                    # Edit all settings
-packnplay configure --section=runtime      # Just container runtime
-packnplay configure --section=credentials  # Just credential settings
-packnplay configure --section=container    # Just default container settings
+packnplay configure                    # Edit all settings in logical flow
 ```
 
 **Safe Editing:**
 - ✅ **Preserves custom settings** not shown in UI (env configs, advanced settings)
 - ✅ **Shows current values** as defaults in forms
-- ✅ **Granular sections** - edit only what you need
+- ✅ **Complete visibility** - see all your current configuration
 - ✅ **No data loss** - manual edits and version tracking are preserved
+- ✅ **Logical flow** - runtime → credentials → default container → update settings
 
 ### Config File
